name: troubleshooting
context: Debug common ESLint errors — config not found, plugin resolution, parser mismatches, flat config migration issues
info: This node covers the most common ESLint errors agents encounter and how to fix them: config file not found, plugin/parser resolution failures, flat config migration issues, conflicting rules, performance problems, and editor integration issues. Each problem includes the error message, root cause, and fix. Agents use this context when ESLint produces unexpected errors, when --fix doesn't work as expected, or when migrating from eslintrc to flat config breaks the configuration.
body:
# ESLint Troubleshooting

Common ESLint problems, their error messages, and how to fix them.

## Config Not Found

**Error:** `ESLint couldn't find a configuration file`

**Cause:** No `eslint.config.js`, `.mjs`, or `.cjs` in the working directory or any parent.

**Fix:**
```bash
# Create a minimal config
npm init @eslint/config@latest

# Or create manually
echo 'import js from "@eslint/js";
export default [js.configs.recommended];' > eslint.config.mjs
npm install --save-dev eslint @eslint/js
```

## Legacy Config Detected

**Error:** `ESLint found a deprecated configuration file (.eslintrc.json). Please use eslint.config.js instead.`

**Fix:**
```bash
# Migrate automatically
npx @eslint/migrate-config .eslintrc.json

# Then remove old files
rm .eslintrc.json .eslintignore
```

See [[eslint/flat-config]] for migration details.

## Plugin Not Found / Failed to Load

**Error:** `Failed to load plugin 'react' declared in ...`

**Cause:** Plugin package not installed, or wrong import in flat config.

**Fix:**
```bash
# Install the missing plugin
npm install --save-dev eslint-plugin-react

# Verify it's imported correctly in eslint.config.mjs
# WRONG: plugins: ["react"]
# RIGHT: import react from "eslint-plugin-react";
#        plugins: { react },
```

## Parser Not Found

**Error:** `Configured parser '@typescript-eslint/parser' was not found`

**Cause:** In flat config, parsers must be imported as objects, not referenced as strings.

**Fix:**
```javascript
// WRONG (eslintrc style)
{ parser: "@typescript-eslint/parser" }

// RIGHT (flat config)
import tseslint from "typescript-eslint";
export default tseslint.config(
  ...tseslint.configs.recommended,
);
```

## No Files Matched

**Error:** `No files matching the pattern "src" were found.`

**Cause:** Glob patterns may need quoting, or the directory doesn't exist.

**Fix:**
```bash
# Quote glob patterns to prevent shell expansion
npx eslint "src/**/*.{js,ts}"

# Or suppress the error
npx eslint --no-error-on-unmatched-pattern "src/**/*.ts"
```

## Rules from Plugin Not Working

**Error:** `Definition for rule 'react/prop-types' was not found`

**Cause:** Plugin not registered in the `plugins` object, or wrong namespace.

**Fix:**
```javascript
// Ensure plugin is registered
import react from "eslint-plugin-react";

export default [
  {
    plugins: { react },  // <-- Must register before using rules
    rules: {
      "react/prop-types": "off",
    },
  },
];
```

## Type-Aware Rules Errors

**Error:** `You have used a rule which requires type information, but don't have parserOptions set to generate type information.`

**Cause:** Type-aware typescript-eslint rules need a tsconfig.

**Fix:**
```javascript
import tseslint from "typescript-eslint";

export default tseslint.config(
  ...tseslint.configs.recommendedTypeChecked,
  {
    languageOptions: {
      parserOptions: {
        projectService: true,
        tsconfigRootDir: import.meta.dirname,
      },
    },
  },
);
```

Make sure `tsconfig.json` includes the files being linted.

## ESLint Too Slow

**Symptoms:** Linting takes >30 seconds for a normal project.

**Common causes and fixes:**

```bash
# 1. Enable caching
npx eslint --cache .

# 2. Check if node_modules is being linted
# Add to config:
{ ignores: ["node_modules/", "dist/", ".git/"] }

# 3. Disable type-aware rules if not needed
# Use tseslint.configs.recommended instead of recommendedTypeChecked

# 4. Debug what's slow
npx eslint --debug . 2>&1 | grep -i "timing"
ESLINT_DEBUG=eslint:* npx eslint .

# 5. Use concurrency (ESLint 9.x)
npx eslint --concurrency auto .
```

## Files Being Ignored Unexpectedly

**Symptoms:** ESLint skips files you expect it to lint.

**Debug:**
```bash
# Check resolved config for a specific file
npx eslint --print-config path/to/file.js

# If empty or error, the file is being ignored
# Check your ignores patterns:
```

**Common causes:**
- Global `ignores` pattern matches the file
- `files` patterns don't include the file extension
- File is in `node_modules/` (always ignored)
- Dotfiles: flat config does NOT ignore dotfiles by default (unlike eslintrc)

## Conflicting Rules (ESLint + Prettier)

**Symptoms:** ESLint and Prettier fight over formatting (semicolons, quotes, etc.).

**Fix:** Install `eslint-config-prettier` and add it LAST:

```javascript
import prettier from "eslint-config-prettier";

export default [
  js.configs.recommended,
  prettier,  // Must be last
];
```

See [[eslint/plugins]] for Prettier integration.

## Debug Commands

```bash
# Print resolved config for a file
npx eslint --print-config src/index.js

# Interactive config inspector
npx eslint --inspect-config

# Full debug output
npx eslint --debug src/

# Environment info
npx eslint --env-info

# Check ESLint version
npx eslint --version
```

## Exit Code 2 (Fatal Error)

**Cause:** Configuration error, not a lint error. Common causes:
- Syntax error in `eslint.config.mjs`
- Missing required dependency
- Invalid rule configuration

**Debug:**
```bash
# Run with debug flag
npx eslint --debug . 2>&1 | head -50

# Validate config syntax
node -e "import('./eslint.config.mjs').then(c => console.log('OK', Object.keys(c.default)))"
```

sections.title: Configuration errors
sections.body: Config not found: create `eslint.config.mjs` or run `npm init @eslint/config`. Legacy config detected: run `npx @eslint/migrate-config .eslintrc.json`. Exit code 2 means fatal config error — check syntax with `--debug`. Use `--print-config <file>` to see resolved config.

sections.title: Plugin and parser errors
sections.body: Plugin not found: install the npm package and import as an object (not string). Parser not found: in flat config, parsers are imported objects in `languageOptions.parser`. Rule not found: ensure the plugin is registered in the `plugins` object. Type-aware rules need `parserOptions.projectService: true` and a valid tsconfig.

sections.title: Performance issues
sections.body: Enable `--cache` to skip unchanged files. Add `node_modules/`, `dist/`, `.git/` to ignores. Type-aware typescript-eslint rules are the biggest perf hit — use `recommended` instead of `recommendedTypeChecked` if speed matters. Use `--concurrency auto` in ESLint 9.x for multi-threaded linting. Debug with `ESLINT_DEBUG=eslint:* npx eslint .`.

sections.title: File matching issues
sections.body: Files ignored unexpectedly: check global `ignores` patterns, `files` globs, and whether the extension is included. Use `--print-config <file>` to check if a file is being linted. Quote glob patterns in shell to prevent expansion: `"src/**/*.ts"`. Use `--no-error-on-unmatched-pattern` when glob may match zero files.

sections.title: Debug commands
sections.body: `--print-config <file>` shows resolved config. `--inspect-config` opens interactive inspector. `--debug` shows full debug output. `--env-info` shows environment and version. `ESLINT_DEBUG=eslint:*` enables debug logging via environment variable.

links.context: Fix config by creating proper eslint.config.mjs
Understand flat config for migration issues

related.note: Many issues stem from eslintrc-to-flat-config migration
Plugin errors are the most common class of ESLint issue
Most troubleshooting starts with verifying the config file

author: dojo-community
